value: Bombus terrestris (worker female); morphological + COI molecular confirmation
Wild-collected, Royal NP NSW (May 2025); 1 commercial colony (Koppert, F2)
n=80 collected; n=72 in analysis (10% pre-trial attrition); a priori power from pilot
30% w/v sucrose ad libitum + commercial pollen; water dish refreshed daily
30x30x30 cm acrylic cages; 24+/-1 C; 60% RH; L:D 12:12; dim red light in dark phase
Heathland, Royal NP NSW (33.9S 151.2E); austral autumn; hand-net only
Daily checks: activity, posture, integument, autotomy
3 deaths over 14 d (suspected handling stress); no protocol change
Foraging substrate + natural debris in cages; handling minimised to <=1x/day
Lethargy and feeding cessation monitored; cold anaesthesia applied precautionarily for handling
Hand-net; <2 h transit in ventilated containers; cold immobilisation for marking
Double-door rearing room; spent animals frozen before disposal
CO2 then freezing (<=-20 C); 30 vouchers deposited at Australian Museum (accession TBD)
No formal review required; welfare reasoning in Methods. NSW collection permit SL101234. IUCN: Least Concern
Endpoint >10% mortality in 48 h (not triggered); no non-target captures
Reduction: paired within-subject design. Refinement: cold anaesthesia for handling. No invasive procedures.
